sections.body: The following action items have been identified from the most recent court orders and servicer communications. Each entry is prioritized according to urgency and includes the associated deadline and responsible owner where specified.

sections.heading: Action Items and Urgent Tasks

blocks.title: Case Management Report Filing Deadline

fields.value: Plaintiff must file a Case Management Report.
Report must include the current status of service on all defendants, any pending motions, estimated time to trial readiness, and whether mediation has been attempted or scheduled.

citations.claim: Plaintiff shall file a Case Management Report no later than ten (10) days prior to the conference. The report shall include: a. Current status of service on all defendants b. Any pending motions c. Estimated time to trial readiness d. Whether mediation has been attempted or scheduled.

spans.raw_text:
3. Plaintiff shall file a Case Management Report no later than
ten (10) days prior to the conference. The report shall
include:
a. Current status of service on all defendants
b. Any pending motions
c. Estimated time to trial readiness
d. Whether mediation has been attempted or scheduled

action_items: Prepare and file the Case Management Report by April 12, 2026.

blocks.title: Proof of Service Filing Deadline

fields.value: File proof of service on all named defendants with the Court.
Plaintiff (or relevant party) must ensure all proofs of service have been completed and filed.

citations.claim: Proof of service on all named defendants must be filed with the Court no later than April 15, 2026.

spans.raw_text:
4. Proof of service on all named defendants must be filed with
the Court no later than April 15, 2026.

action_items: File proof of service on all named defendants by April 15, 2026.

blocks.title: Case Management Conference

fields.value: All parties must appear in person before Judge Patricia Navarro.
Attend the Case Management Conference at Courtroom 5-3, Miami-Dade County Courthouse, 73 W Flagler St, Miami, FL 33130. Failure to appear may result in sanctions, including striking of pleadings.

citations.claim: A Case Management Conference is set for April 22, 2026 at 10:00 AM before the Honorable Judge Patricia Navarro, Courtroom 5-3, Miami-Dade County Courthouse, 73 W Flagler St, Miami, FL 33130. All parties shall appear. Failure to appear may result in sanctions, including striking of pleadings.

sections.body: The current payoff amount for the loan is $490,527.44 as of March 22, 2026, based on the latest servicer communication, reflecting accrued interest, late charges, and a newly posted corporate advance. This represents an increase from the prior quoted amount of $487,920.00 as of March 1, 2026.

blocks.notes: Payoff updated from $487,920.00 (Mar 1, 2026) to $490,527.44 (Mar 22, 2026) to include accrued interest, late charges, and a corporate advance for hazard insurance.
blocks.title: Updated Payoff Amount

fields.value: Servicer Email (March 22, 2026, Marcus Bell, Wells Fargo Servicing)

citations.claim: Current payoff is $490,527.44 as of March 22, 2026.

spans.raw_text:
Please note that the payoff amount has been revised. The current
payoff is now $490,527.44 as of March 22, 2026 (up from $487,920.00
quoted on March 15). The increase reflects accrued interest, late
charges, and a corporate advance posted on March 18 for force-placed
hazard insurance that our prior quote did not include.

sections.body: This section addresses the recent servicing transfer for the subject loan and details for borrower's counsel. The servicing of the loan for Carlos A. Rodriguez has been transferred from Wells Fargo to Nationstar Mortgage LLC d/b/a Mr. Cooper, effective April 1, 2026. All post-transfer correspondence should be directed to Mr. Cooper. Counsel for the borrower is Midwest Legal Partners, LLP.

blocks.notes: All correspondence after April 1, 2026, must be sent to Mr. Cooper as per the March 15, 2026 notification.

sections.body: Summary of key title concerns impacting the case, including current liens, recent tax delinquency designations, HOA lis pendens, and other recorded encumbrances. Each item below is cited to source title documents.

blocks.notes: Notice of Lis Pendens filed by the HOA for unpaid assessments. This is a critical encumbrance; party-naming and response required for foreclosure posture.
blocks.title: HOA Lis Pendens for Unpaid Assessments

citations.claim: Notice of Lis Pendens filed by PALMETTO BAY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, INC. on January 22, 2026 in the amount of $3,420.00 for unpaid assessments, recorded as Instrument No. 2026-0034567.

spans.raw_text:
[L20] 4. Notice of Lis Pendens filed by PALMETTO BAY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION,
[L21] INC. on January 22, 2026 in the amount of $3,420.00 for unpaid
[L22] assessments, recorded as Instrument No. 2026-0034567.

action_items: Review and address the HOA's lis pendens in current proceedings; determine implications for party-naming and potential priority.

blocks.notes: 2025 property taxes are currently unpaid and have been reported as delinquent. These taxes are a superior lien on the property.
blocks.title: 2025 Property Taxes – Unpaid and Delinquent Status Reported

citations.claim: 2025 property taxes in the amount of $8,247.00 are a lien, unpaid, and delinquent as of the due date November 1, 2025.

[L9] which are a lien but not yet due and payable.
[L10] Amount: $8,247.00
[L11] Tax Parcel No.: 33-5022-014-0290

action_items: Immediate assessment and payoff calculation for 2025 taxes; confirm actual status with Miami-Dade tax office.
